summary: High‑energy second set with epic Standing on the Moon
blurb: The show shines in the second set, delivering a soaring Standing On The Moon and tight jams through Eyes Of The World and Crazy Fingers. Jerry’s vocals and guitar are especially vivid, while Phil lays down fire‑hot bass throughout.
review:
Recorded on March 31, 1994 at The Omni, this Dead show is a classic case of a sluggish first set rescued by an electrifying second half. While the opening numbers – Touch Of Grey through Easy Answers – feel flat and suffer from mixed mix quality, the band truly comes alive after the intermission. Jerry Garcia commands the stage with soulful vocals and searing leads on Crazy Fingers and a dramatic reprise of Standing On The Moon; his connection with the crowd is palpable. Phil Lesh rides a wave of inspiration, “on fire” during Eyes Of The World and underpinning the extended jams with inventive bass lines. The second set flows like a river, moving from a tight Eyes Of The World into Saint Of Circumstance, then launching into a fiery Crazy Fingers → Corinna segue, seamless Drums‑Space‑The Last Time sequence, and culminating in an emotional Standing On The Moon that leaves the audience breathless. Though the encore (I Fought The Law) is brief, the night’s high points more than compensate for the first‑set woes, making this a must‑listen performance for any Deadhead.

For listeners seeking the clearest window into this night, the crystal‑clear soundboard capture (gd1994-03-31.sbd.chaser.tetzeli.fix-10930.34841.reflac.flac16) offers warm detail and full audience ambience, preserving the band’s high‑energy spring run without audible dropouts. Fans with a tolerance for mixed first‑set audio may also enjoy the Miller SBD version, which showcases the second set’s magic despite its uneven early mix.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings describe the show as mixed, while two are clearly positive, highlighting strong performances (Jerry’s vocals/guitar, Phil’s bass) and excellent soundboard quality. The prevalence of positive language across multiple sources justifies a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Warm, crystal‑clear soundboard captures the high‑energy performance with full detail and no noticeable flaws.

key_highlights: Second set peaks with an epic Standing On The Moon and seamless Drums‑Space‑The Last Time flow.
Jerry’s vocal reprise of Standing On The Moon and searing solos on Crazy Fingers stand out.
Phil delivers a fire‑hot bass line on Eyes Of The World, driving the jam sections.
Recording 3 provides warm, detailed soundboard audio that makes the show a must‑listen despite an uneven first set.

band_performance.Jerry: High‑energy connection with the crowd; vocal reprise of Standing On The Moon and soulful, searing guitar solos on Crazy Fingers and SOTM.
band_performance.Phil: "On fire" bass work, especially on Eyes Of The World, adding creativity throughout extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Some listeners sensed a hint of frustration after the second set climax, though opinions vary.
